name: Sa'dah
description: Yemen's most intact rammed-earth walled city. The zabur tradition — coursed clay compacted between shuttered forms — creates ochre-toned walls of extraordinary thermal performance. The city's defensive ring wall and Friday mosque are the defining monuments.
traditional: Rammed-earth walls in 30cm courses, earthen minaret, flat roofs with crenelated parapets.
transitional: Stabilised rammed earth with lime admixture, traditional coursing pattern retained.
contemporary: Contemporary Sa'dah: rammed-earth aesthetic with structural reinforcement, passive cooling.
